---
title: "Module 01: Foundations and Generalization"
---

## Learning objectives

- Frame **supervised learning** (predicting an outcome \(y\) from measured predictors \(x\)) with a clear **train / validation / test** story.
- Connect **bias–variance** and “too wiggly vs too stiff” to plots students already see on Monday.
- Start the habit of a **data card**: what was measured, who is missing, what you would not claim from a score alone.

[← Module library](index.qmd) · [Next: Module 02 — Regularization →](module-02-regularization.qmd)

---

## On the slides (Day 1)

| Block | Topic | Slide link |
|-------|--------|------------|
| Part A | R, ggplot, tools | [Day 1 deck](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd) (opening) |
| Part B | `lm`, assumptions, Hald cement | [Day 1 deck](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd) |
| Part C | Gene × trait, overfitting, lasso | [Part C](../slides/day-01-monday.html#/part-c-gene-trait) |
| Part D | Gene × disease, logistic lasso | [Part D](../slides/day-01-monday.html#/part-d-gene-disease) |

---

## Key messages

- **Simple baselines** (linear models, clear splits) anchor everything that comes later.
- Two data tracks: **simulated genes** (known truth) and **[Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)** (one real table all week).

---

## Supervised setup

- Rows are units (birds, patients, assays); columns are outcomes and predictors.
- In compact notation, predictors are \(X \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times p}\), outcomes are \(y\), and we learn a rule \(\hat f(X) \rightarrow y\).
- **Generalization** means performance on **new** rows from a similar process — not memorizing the training table.

---

## Splits and honest evaluation

- **Training** fits the model; **validation** (or CV) picks complexity; **test** is touched **once** for a final score.
- Any step that **learns from data** (means, dummy levels, PCA, imputation) must be **re-fit inside each CV fold** — see [Module 04](module-04-pipeline.qmd) and [Day 2 pipeline](../slides/day-02-tuesday.html#/part-c-pipeline).

### Leakage checklist (30 seconds)

Before you trust any metric, ask:

1. Did I split before any learned preprocessing?
2. Are repeated measures grouped correctly in CV?
3. Did I tune only on training/CV rows (not test)?
4. Is the final number from untouched test data?

### Word example (train vs validation)

Imagine 100 penguins for tuning bill-length cutoffs. You fit thresholds on **80 birds** (training). You score **20 held-out** birds (validation). If you had tuned the cutoff by trying every value on **all 100** and then reported accuracy on those same 100, you would be **optimistic** — the model saw the answers while you chose the rule.

---

## Bias and variance

| Pattern | Train error | Validation error | Plain language |
|---------|-------------|------------------|----------------|
| Underfit | High | High | Too simple (e.g. intercept-only null model on genes) |
| Overfit | Low | High | Too flexible for sample size \(n\) (deep tree, tiny leaf size) |
| Good tradeoff | Moderate | Moderate | Validation guides complexity |

- **Gene trait (Monday):** null model underfits; full OLS can overfit when many gene effects are estimated from limited \(n\).  
- **Penguins (Tuesday+):** a stump (`tree_depth = 1`) underfits; a very deep tree can chase noise — tuning `tree_depth` / `min_n` on **CV accuracy** gives a better bias-variance tradeoff ([Day 2](../slides/day-02-tuesday.html#/part-c-pipeline)).

---

## Data card (lightweight)

Document on one page:

1. **Provenance** — where rows came from.  
2. **Outcome definition** — what “positive” means clinically or biologically.  
3. **Missingness** — what is NA and why.  
4. **Leakage risks** — repeated measures, future information, imputation before split.  
5. **Subgroup fields** — species, island, sex (penguins) or batch (omics).

The [curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd) asks students to draft this Monday and finish the **model card** Thursday.

---

## Course links

- **Slides:** [Day 1 (Monday)](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd)
- **Gene labs:** [linear-regression-lasso.Rmd](../notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd), [logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd](../notebooks/logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd)
- **Penguin notebooks:** [Notebook index](../notebooks/index.qmd)
- **Regularization (next):** [Module 02](module-02-regularization.qmd)
- **Data:** [Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)
